reasoning: As of early 2025, exactly 48 countries have ratified the AfCFTA. The 48th country was Liberia in August 2024. Libya remains one of the remaining six holdouts, alongside countries like Sudan, South Sudan, and Somalia. Libya is in a state of extended political crisis, with competing governments in the East and West. No recent news indicates that Libya's parliament has even discussed, let alone scheduled a vote, to ratify the AfCFTA agreement. Furthermore, only two months remain between the forecast date (May 1, 2025) and the resolution date (July 1, 2025), leaving virtually no time for a divided government to negotiate and finalize ratification of a major international trade treaty. With no evidence of any preliminary steps taken, the probability of complete ratification by July 1, 2025 is exceedingly low.
